rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe painful plaque flare with immobility needs urgent same-day in-person assessment (analgesia, exclude erythroderma/infection) and an urgent dermatology referral, but without current emergency features — T3, escalating for erythroderma/pustular flare/sepsis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Whole-body bright-red skin, widespread new pustules, high fever/rigors, or inability to keep warm/feeling freezing
escalation_edges.rationale: Erythrodermic/pustular psoriasis is a life-threatening dermatological emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your whole body turns bright red, you get widespread pustules, a high fever with shivering, or you feel freezing and can't keep warm — call 000 or go to the emergency department immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Thick yellow pus, spreading hot redness, or fever from the cracked skin
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary bacterial infection/sepsis through fissured skin.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the cracks leak thick yellow pus, the redness spreads and gets hot, or you develop a fever — seek urgent/ED care.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Spouse to help with emollient application and monitor for emergency features; ensure the dermatology referral proceeds.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Apply heavy greasy moisturiser all over several times a day and the steroid ointment to the worst plaques, take pain relief, and don't pick the scales. You need an urgent dermatology referral for stronger treatment. Watch for whole-body redness, high fever/shivering or pus.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP for pain/assessment + urgent dermatology referral; ED/000 for emergency features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for pain/assessment; ED/000 for erythroderma, pustular flare or sepsis.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a severe strep-triggered plaque psoriasis flare (erythroderma/pustular/sepsis screened negative), emollient/topical therapy, and urgent dermatology referral.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Simple topical advice with no erythroderma/sepsis screen or dermatology referral.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving erythrodermic/pustular psoriasis or secondary sepsis is missed and the patient decompensates.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays analgesia/emollient therapy and specialist referral for a severe flare.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for a stable plaque flare without erythroderma/pustular/sepsis features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot accurately estimate BSA or detect early pustulation/erythroderma remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the severe flare with emollients/potent topical/analgesia and urgent dermatology referral, and give an explicit erythroderma/pustular/sepsis safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Topicals-only advice, or missing an emergency variant.
